verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: Acquire + clean-release semantics written; Little-brother-always-functions invariant stated; Consumed by [[tasks/yzx-iso/t8-0-lane-index]]; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gates committed red at lifeos a82c166 (4/4 failing: spec absent), green at HEAD (4/4)
gate_result.acquire_evidence: Spec section 1 defines on-demand acquisition through the declared control plane with the prior host state recorded before control is taken (I11), and clean-release that restores the prior host state exactly as recorded, reversible and time-bounded (I12).
gate_result.invariant_evidence: The little-brother-always-functions invariant is stated as normative I13: the host OS always functions normally — it updates, reboots, and runs its own daemons without LifeOS interference — with the 2026-07-21 drdave path as empirical proof.
gate_result.consumer_evidence: Section 1 is declared consumed by tasks/yzx-iso/t8-0-lane-index, and all ten yzx-iso lane indexes were updated in GitKB to reference the ratified spec.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: tests/archbp-058-two-brother-model.spec.ts executes structural validation against the published planning-spine-v0/docs/isolation-architecture-spec.md — the real, versioned artifact, not a fixture.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-058 is complete: the two-brother control model is documented as the normative section 1 of the ratified isolation architecture spec v1.0.0 — LifeOS (big brother) acquires host resources on demand through a declared, audited control plane with prior-state recording, and every acquire has a clean, reversible release that restores the recorded prior host state; the little-brother-always-functions invariant (I13) is stated normatively with the 2026-07-21 empirical basis. The section is consumed by the t8 host-control lane and the spec is referenced by all ten yzx-iso lane indexes.
